description_long: This is a large, four-engine military-style airplane with a long, cylindrical fuselage and a straight, high-mounted wing configuration. The wings are broad and feature four propeller engines, two on each side, with visible propeller blades. The tail section is equipped with a twin vertical stabilizer (H-tail) and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The exterior is painted in a camouflage pattern with shades of green, brown, and gray, and the cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage with a clear canopy. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable or not modeled in detail. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era heavy bombers, built primarily from metal with glass for the cockpit windows.
description: A large, four-engine military-style airplane with a camouflage exterior, high-mounted straight wings, and a twin vertical stabilizer tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted with a clear canopy, and the design is reminiscent of a World War II heavy bomber.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad wingspan, four propeller engines, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical fuselage, high-mounted wings, and the twin vertical stabilizer tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the propeller engines and camouflage pattern along the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy at the front of the fuselage.
